nodes.text: Good evening. Take a breath. Let's understand your day together — honestly.
When something you planned today didn't work out — what was your first thought?
options: It's okay, maybe next time
I could have done something differently
It wasn't in my control at all
I felt stuck and couldn't move on easily

options.condition: answer=It's okay, maybe next time|I could have done something differently
answer=It wasn't in my control at all|I felt stuck and couldn't move on easily

nodes.text: When things went differently than planned — did you look for something you could still do?
options: Yes — I found a small way to move forward
I accepted it and kept going
I thought about it but didn't act
Not really — I just let it go

nodes.text: When today felt out of your hands — did any part of it feel like your call?
options: Looking back, yes — I had at least one choice
Maybe — I'm not sure yet
Not really — it was mostly outside me
No — someone else controlled the outcome

nodes.text: You didn't let an unplanned moment define your day. You looked for what was still in your hands — and that's a quiet kind of strength. Not everything needs to go right for you to show up right.

Some days the circumstances really do take over. That's real. But even in the middle of that — you noticed. And noticing is where agency begins. Tomorrow, see if you can catch that moment one step earlier.
Now let's move from how you handled things — to what you gave today.
Think of a moment today where you helped someone or did something extra. What was going through your mind?
options: It felt right — I didn't need a reason
I helped but quietly hoped someone would notice
I waited to see if someone else would step up first
I felt others should have done it instead of me

options.condition: answer=It felt right — I didn't need a reason
answer=I helped but quietly hoped someone would notice|I waited to see if someone else would step up first|I felt others should have done it instead of me

nodes.text: When you helped — did you think about whether they would return the favour someday?
options: No — that thought didn't come
Briefly, but it didn't change what I did
Yes — I hoped they would remember
I wasn't sure what I expected

nodes.text: When your effort goes unnoticed — how does that usually sit with you?
options: It bothers me more than I'd like to admit
I remind myself the work matters regardless
I start wondering if it was worth it
I feel okay — I know my own value

nodes.text: You gave without keeping score. That's rarer than it sounds — most people help with at least one eye on the ledger. The fact that you move toward need without calculating return is something worth carrying forward.
Wanting to be seen for what you give — that's human, not shameful. The question is whether that need is driving your choices or just following them. Today, just notice which it was.
One last shift — from what you gave, to who you kept in mind today.
When you had a disagreement or difficult conversation today — what were you mostly thinking about?
options: Making sure my point was heard
Whether the other person might actually be right
How this situation was affecting the other person
How to end the conversation quickly

options.condition: answer=Whether the other person might actually be right|How this situation was affecting the other person
answer=Making sure my point was heard|How to end the conversation quickly

nodes.text: When you realized the other person might be right — what did you do with that feeling?
options: I said so — I told them they had a point
I held it quietly but it changed how I responded
I sat with it but didn't act on it yet
I pushed past it to finish making my point

nodes.text: After the conversation ended — did you think about how the other person was feeling?
options: Yes — I wondered if I had been fair to them
Briefly — then moved on
Not really — I was focused on my own feelings
Only if they seemed visibly upset

nodes.text: You made room for the other person — even in the middle of disagreement. That's not weakness, that's perspective. It means you're in conversation, not just in combat. That quality keeps relationships intact when most fall apart.

Today your focus stayed close — on your own side of things. That's not wrong. Sometimes you need to be your own advocate. But notice — is there someone from today whose experience you haven't considered yet?
Here is what today showed about you.
Rest well. You showed up today — that counts.

axis1.internal: When plans broke down, you looked for what was still yours to do. That's agency — quiet, steady, and real.
axis1.external: Today felt out of your hands. The circumstances were real. And somewhere in there was still a choice — even a small one.

axis2.contribution: You gave without keeping score today. That's the kind of effort that holds teams together — even when no one's watching.
axis2.entitlement: You wanted to be seen for what you gave. That's human. The work now is making sure giving comes before the wanting.

axis3.altrocentric: You kept other people in your awareness today — even when it wasn't easy. That's a wider radius than most people manage.
axis3.self_centric: Your focus stayed on your own experience today. That has its place. Tomorrow, try widening the lens by just one person.
